frameworks.name: task-state representation in OFC
frameworks.one_line: lOFC encodes abstract task variables (reward history) that drive adaptive sequential biases

frameworks.name: sequential / history-dependent choice
frameworks.one_line: past outcomes bias current choices; this paper dissociates risky-bias from spatial win-stay/lose-shift

frameworks.name: value-based decision-making
frameworks.one_line: choices between cued guaranteed vs probabilistic rewards as the behavioral substrate

core_question: Does the lateral OFC support trial-by-trial learning of all sequential biases, or only abstract task-specific ones like risk preference?
key_assumptions: trial outcomes are statistically independent so any sequential effect is endogenous
lOFC encodes task-state variables including reward history
optogenetic inhibition during choice reporting isolates lOFC contribution to updating
manipulations: explicitly cued guaranteed vs probabilistic reward options
optogenetic inhibition of lOFC time-locked to choice reporting
trial-type contrast: risky-win history vs spatial win-stay/lose-shift
key_findings: lOFC neurons strongly encode reward history and receipt
behavior shows increased risk-taking after risky wins and spatial win-stay/lose-shift biases
lOFC inhibition eliminates the post-risky-win increase in risk preference
lOFC inhibition spares spatial sequential biases
interpretations: different sequential biases are neurally dissociable
lOFC supports learning of abstract task-specific biases but not spatial ones, consistent with OFC representing position in a task-state space
